items.title: Image compression
items.scenario: A grayscale image is stored as an m×n pixel matrix A. Keeping only the top k singular values in SVD reconstructs a smooth low-rank approximation that uses far less storage while preserving major structure.
items.explanation: Large σᵢ capture broad intensity patterns; small σᵢ often correspond to fine noise. Truncation is lossy compression with a principled error bound.

items.title: Latent factors in recommendations
items.scenario: A users×items rating matrix R is sparse. Truncated SVD approximates R ≈ U_k Σ_k V_kᵀ, where rows of U_k are user embeddings and rows of V_k are item embeddings in k latent dimensions.
items.explanation: Low-rank structure assumes preferences are driven by a few hidden factors (genre, mood, price sensitivity)—similar in spirit to modern embedding models.

items.title: PCA via SVD on centered data
items.scenario: A team centers feature matrix X (subtract column means) and runs SVD. Right singular vectors V are principal directions; singular values relate to explained variance.
items.explanation: For centered X, PCA eigenvectors of covariance match right singular vectors of X; this avoids explicitly forming the covariance matrix when n is large.
